breadcrumbs (was MISSING in Cycles 2 & 3 — wait_for_selector added but never validated due to CDP crash)

skipped_reasons.B0BF1J7C7W: HTTP 404 — dead product URL, excluded from quality assessment

results.validation_data_source: Live runs in Cycle 4: output_2026-06-17_181813.json (B09V3KXJPB) and output_2026-06-17_180228.json from Cycle 2 (B07VGRJDFY). B07VGRJDFY timed out consistently in Cycle 4 due to networkidle timeout issue.

id.notes: Sequential numbering. B09V3KXJPB=id=1, B07VGRJDFY=id=3 (gap for dead URL).

title.notes: B09V3KXJPB: 'Apple iPad Air (5th Generation): with M1 chip, 10.9-inch Liquid Retina Display, 64GB, Wi-Fi 6, 12MP front/12MP Back Camera, Touch ID, All-Day Battery Life – Pink' — complete and correct. B07VGRJDFY: 'Nintendo Switch with Neon Blue and Neon Red Joy‑Con' — correct.

price.notes: B09V3KXJPB: empty (Out of Stock — correct, no price shown). B07VGRJDFY: empty (Unknown availability — acceptable). 6-level price fallback chain correctly implemented per product_analysis.json. Still NOT tested on an in-stock product after 4 cycles.

availability.notes: B09V3KXJPB: 'Out of Stock' — CORRECT. B07VGRJDFY: 'Unknown' — acceptable fallback when no add-to-cart/outOfStock indicators found.

original_price.notes: Empty for both products. Neither is on sale, so empty is correct. Selectors (.basisPrice .a-offscreen, .a-price.a-text-price .a-offscreen, tr td.a-text-strike .a-offscreen) correctly implemented with per-unit price filtering.

url.notes: B09V3KXJPB: 'https://www.amazon.com/dp/B09V3KXJPB'. B07VGRJDFY: 'https://www.amazon.com/dp/B07VGRJDFY'. Clean ASIN-based URLs, correctly normalized.

remarks.notes: B0BF1J7C7W (404): 'No product title found — page may be invalid or blocked' — correct. Successful products: empty string — correct.

asin.notes: B09V3KXJPB and B07VGRJDFY correctly extracted from URL and hidden input.

rating.notes: B09V3KXJPB: '4.8 out of 5 stars'. B07VGRJDFY: '4.8 out of 5 stars'. Correct format.

review_count.notes: B09V3KXJPB: '(13,483)'. B07VGRJDFY: '(80,819)'. Correct format with parentheses.

brand.notes: B09V3KXJPB: 'Apple' — CORRECT. B07VGRJDFY: '' — STILL MISSING despite 5 fallback levels added. The code now tries #bylineInfo, a.bylineInfo, table.prodDetTable, #brand, a[href*='/brand/'], and .a-row .a-link-normal[href*='store']. None match the Nintendo Switch page. Nintendo's byline structure may use a non-standard class or the element may not be present.

breadcrumbs.notes: STILL EMPTY after 3 cycles of fixes. The wait_for_selector('#wayfinding-breadcrumbs_container a', timeout=5000) was added and does not throw (suggesting it either finds elements or times out silently in the try/except), but the JS extraction returns []. The issue is likely that Amazon's breadcrumb container either: (a) uses a different selector structure that doesn't match #wayfinding-breadcrumbs_container a, (b) the links are in shadow DOM, (c) the container is empty/absent on some pages, or (d) the wait_for_selector finds the container but the a elements are not direct children. The fallback selectors .a-breadcrumb a and nav.a-breadcrumb a also return empty. This was the primary retest target for Cycle 3-4 and remains unfixed.

description.notes: B09V3KXJPB: '' — empty (Out of Stock, description may not render). B07VGRJDFY: full multi-paragraph description extracted — CORRECT.

feature_bullets.notes: B09V3KXJPB: 9 bullet items. B07VGRJDFY: 5 bullet items. Content matches expected format.

issues.problem: Breadcrumbs still empty after 3 cycles of attempted fixes (Cycles 2-4)
issues.details: The wait_for_selector('#wayfinding-breadcrumbs_container a', timeout=5000) was added at line 323-326 in scrape_product(). The JS extraction has 3 levels of fallback selectors (#wayfinding-breadcrumbs_container a, #wayfinding-breadcrumbs_container ul li a, .a-breadcrumb a / nav.a-breadcrumb a). Despite all this, breadcrumbs return [] on both products. The wait does not throw an error, which means either: (1) the selector matches but returns no <a> elements, (2) the try/except catches the timeout and the JS runs before breadcrumbs render, or (3) Amazon's current DOM uses a different breadcrumb structure than documented. Since the scraper can't be debugged live (Playwright in container), the root cause cannot be determined without a live DOM inspection.
issues.suggested_fix: Try a broader breadcrumb detection approach: after the wait, log document.querySelector('#wayfinding-breadcrumbs_container')?.innerHTML to see what's in the container. If the container exists but has no <a> children, the breadcrumbs may be inside a component/iframe. Alternative: try page.query_selector_all('#wayfinding-breadcrumbs_container *') to see if any elements exist at all. If nothing works, accept that breadcrumbs are unreliable on Amazon and set them to empty gracefully — this is an EXTRA field, not a core requirement.

issues.problem: wait_until='networkidle' causes consistent timeouts on some Amazon product pages (B07VGRJDFY)
issues.details: B07VGRJDFY (Nintendo Switch) consistently times out with 'Page.goto: Timeout 30000ms exceeded' when using wait_until='networkidle'. Amazon pages load many tracking scripts, ads, and analytics that keep network connections open indefinitely. This prevents networkidle from ever triggering. The same page loaded successfully in Cycle 2 (possibly with different network conditions). The 30s timeout is too short for networkidle on heavy Amazon pages.
issues.suggested_fix: Change wait_until from 'networkidle' to 'domcontentloaded' in scrape_product() line ~308. This waits for the DOM to be ready but doesn't wait for all network requests to finish. The explicit waits for #productTitle and #wayfinding-breadcrumbs_container already handle lazy-loaded content. This was suggested in Cycle 3 feedback but not yet implemented. Code change: page.goto(url, wait_until='domcontentloaded', timeout=PAGE_LOAD_TIMEOUT)

issues.problem: Brand still empty for B07VGRJDFY (Nintendo Switch) despite 5 fallback levels
issues.details: The code now has 5 fallback selectors: #bylineInfo, a.bylineInfo, table.prodDetTable, #brand, a[href*='/brand/'], and .a-row .a-link-normal[href*='store']. None matched for the Nintendo Switch page. The Nintendo product page likely has a byline with 'Visit the Nintendo Store' but the specific DOM element doesn't match any of the 5 selectors.
issues.suggested_fix: Add a more generic byline regex: extract any text matching 'Visit the (.*?) Store' from the entire page body, or check for #bylineInfo span, #brandByline, or .a-section.a-spacing-none .a-size-base.a-link-normal. Brand is an EXTRA field — low priority.

issues.problem: Price extraction never validated on an in-stock product after 4 cycles
issues.details: B09V3KXJPB is Out of Stock (empty price correct). B07VGRJDFY had Unknown availability. No in-stock product has been tested. The 6-level price fallback chain is correctly implemented but unvalidated on a product with visible price.
issues.suggested_fix: Add a known in-stock product URL to input_urls.json for testing, e.g., B0CHWRXH8B (Amazon Basics) or any currently in-stock popular product.

issues.problem: original_price extraction not tested on a sale product
issues.details: No sale product has been tested across any cycle. The selectors are correctly implemented.
issues.suggested_fix: No code fix needed. Test with a product known to be on sale.

edge_case_notes.out_of_stock: B09V3KXJPB correctly detected as Out of Stock with empty price — correct behavior.
edge_case_notes.dead_url_handling: B0BF1J7C7W returned HTTP 404, empty title, appropriate remarks — correctly handled and excluded from quality assessment.
edge_case_notes.captcha_handling: CAPTCHA detection logic present. No CAPTCHA triggered during Cycle 4 runs.
edge_case_notes.cdp_fallback: FIXED in Cycle 4. The try/except around connect_over_cdp works perfectly. Logger shows: 'CDP connection failed (...), falling back to local Chromium'. Local Chromium launches and pages load successfully.
edge_case_notes.timeout_handling: FAIL: B07VGRJDFY consistently times out with networkidle. Should use domcontentloaded instead.

script_checks.notes: All structural checks pass. CDP fallback is now correctly implemented with try/except. Breadcrumb wait is added but extraction returns empty. networkidle causes timeouts on heavy pages.

cdp_fallback.fix_applied: try/except around connect_over_cdp with fallback to local Chromium launch
cdp_fallback.evidence: Logger output: 'CDP connection failed (BrowserType.connect_over_cdp: socket hang up), falling back to local Chromium'. Scraper then successfully launches Chromium and scrapes B09V3KXJPB.

breadcrumbs_wait.evidence: Both B09V3KXJPB and B07VGRJDFY return breadcrumbs: []. The wait doesn't throw (caught by try/except), but the selector finds no <a> elements in the container.

brand_fallback_4.fix_applied: Added Fallback 4: .a-row .a-link-normal[href*='store'] selector for 'Visit the {Brand} Store' pattern
brand_fallback_4.evidence: B07VGRJDFY still returns brand: ''. The Nintendo Switch page doesn't have elements matching any of the 5 fallback selectors.

confidence_notes: Confidence 0.85 based on: (1) All core fields (id, title, price, availability, currency, url, src_url, status_code, scraped_at, remarks) are CORRECT, (2) All extra fields except breadcrumbs and brand are CORRECT (asin, rating, review_count, image, description, feature_bullets), (3) CDP fallback bug FIXED — scraper now runs reliably, (4) Breadcrumbs remain broken after 3 cycles (medium severity — extra field), (5) networkidle timeout causes scraping failures on some pages (medium severity — reliability issue), (6) Brand fails for Nintendo edge case (low severity). If breadcrumbs are accepted as unreliable (extra field) and networkidle is changed to domcontentloaded, confidence would be 0.92+.

feedback_for_writer.summary: CDP crash bug is FIXED — the scraper runs again. However, 2 medium issues remain: (1) breadcrumbs still empty after 3 cycles of fixes, (2) networkidle timeout causes failures on some pages. 1 low issue: brand fails for Nintendo edge case.

page_goto_wait_until.issue: wait_until='networkidle' causes consistent 30s timeouts on heavy Amazon pages (B07VGRJDFY). networkidle waits for ALL network connections to close — Amazon's tracking/ad scripts keep connections open indefinitely.
page_goto_wait_until.fix:
In scrape_product(), change:
    response = page.goto(url, wait_until='networkidle', timeout=PAGE_LOAD_TIMEOUT)
to:
    response = page.goto(url, wait_until='domcontentloaded', timeout=PAGE_LOAD_TIMEOUT)
The explicit waits for #productTitle and #wayfinding-breadcrumbs_container already handle lazy-loaded content. domcontentloaded fires when DOM is parsed, which is sufficient since the scraper waits for specific elements anyway.
page_goto_wait_until.reason: This causes actual scraping failures. B07VGRJDFY timed out in 3 consecutive attempts in Cycle 4, preventing brand/breadcrumb validation.

breadcrumbs.issue: Empty after 3 cycles despite wait_for_selector and 3 fallback selectors. Either Amazon's DOM doesn't use #wayfinding-breadcrumbs_container a, or the container is empty/absent.
breadcrumbs.fix:
Try one of these approaches:
1. After the wait, log page.query_selector('#wayfinding-breadcrumbs_container').inner_text() to debug what's in the container
2. Try broader selectors: '#cr-breadcrumbs', '.a-breadcrumb .a-list-item', '#wayfinding-breadcrumbs_container span'
3. If breadcrumbs truly cannot be extracted reliably, remove the wait_for_selector (it wastes 5s per product) and accept empty breadcrumbs gracefully — this is an EXTRA field, not core.
Code: Remove or reduce the wait to 2000ms if breadcrumbs remain unreliable.
breadcrumbs.reason: Breadcrumbs have been the retest focus for 3 cycles with no improvement. If the DOM structure doesn't support it, move on.

brand.issue: Brand empty for Nintendo Switch despite 5 fallback selectors
brand.reason: Brand works for Apple and most products. Nintendo is an edge case with non-standard byline markup.

code_suggestions: HIGH PRIORITY: Change wait_until from 'networkidle' to 'domcontentloaded' in page.goto() call. This will fix the timeout issue and also speed up scraping significantly (current networkidle adds ~15-20s per page vs ~5s for domcontentloaded).
MEDIUM: Either fix breadcrumbs with broader selectors/debugging, or remove the 5s breadcrumb wait to save time per product (accept empty as graceful degradation for an extra field).
LOW: Add text regex fallback for brand extraction: /Visit the ([\w\s]+) Store/ pattern from page body text.
TESTING: Add a known in-stock product to input_urls.json to finally validate price extraction.
